mnemonics.title: IoT Components Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember S-C-U-A: Sensors collect, Connectivity carries, User Interface displays, Actuators act.

mnemonics.title: IoT Categories Recall
mnemonics.content: CCI - Consumer, Commercial, Industrial - each serves different purposes.

mnemonics.title: Connectivity Types
mnemonics.content: Short range: Wi-Fi, BT, Zigbee. Long range: LoRa, Cellular, LPWAN.

acronyms.usage: Direct communication between devices without human intervention
Long-range, low-power connectivity for IoT devices
Web transfer protocol for constrained devices
Enables communication between IoT applications and devices

visualTips.visualization: Draw a 4-layer pyramid: Bottom=Sensors/Devices, 2nd=Connectivity, 3rd=Data Processing, Top=Application/User Interface. Each layer supports the one above.
Draw an exponential curve starting slow (2010), rising steeply (2020-2025), and projecting even higher (2030). Label key milestones: 10B (2020), 15B (2023), 30B (2030).
Create a pie chart showing: Consumer IoT ~45%, Industrial IoT ~30%, Commercial IoT ~15%, Infrastructure IoT ~10%.

analogies.analogy: Like a nervous system - sensors are nerves detecting stimuli, connectivity is the spinal cord transmitting signals, the cloud is the brain processing information, and actuators are muscles taking action.
Sensors are like human senses - eyes (cameras), ears (microphones), skin (temperature sensors), nose (gas sensors) - all gathering information about the environment.
Like a reflex action - instead of waiting for the brain (cloud) to process, the spinal cord (edge) reacts immediately for time-critical decisions.
